company_profile.name: Recursion Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Recursion Pharmaceuticals, Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company, engages in the decoding biology and chemistry by integrating technological innovations across biology, chemistry, automation, data science, and engineering to industrialize drug discovery in the United States and the United Kingdom. The company develops REC-4881, which is in Phase 1b/2 clinical trial to treat familial adenomatous polyposis; REC-617, which is in Phase 1/2 clinical trial to treat advanced solid tumors; REC-1245, which is in Phase 1/2 clinical trial to treat biomarker-enriched solid tumors and lymphoma; REC-3565 that is in Phase 1/2 for the treatment of relapsed or refractory B-cell malignancies; and REC-4539, a reversible, CNS penetrant, orally bioavailable, and potential best-in-class inhibitor of LSD1 for the treatment of solid tumors and hematology oncology. Its preclinical stage product includes REC-7735 for the treatment of HR+ breast cancer; and REC-102 for the treatment of hypophosphatasia. The company has collaboration and agreement with Roche & Genentech; Sanofi; Bayer AG; Tempus; and Takeda Pharmaceutical Company Limited. Recursion Pharmaceuticals, Inc. was incorporated in 2013 and is headquartered in Salt Lake City, Utah.
company_profile.description_summary:
A clinical-stage biotechnology company developing multiple oncology and rare disease drug candidates through integrated technological platforms spanning biology, chemistry, automation, data science, and engineering. Currently advancing five clinical-stage programs targeting cancers and genetic disorders, with preclinical candidates in development for breast cancer and metabolic disease.

The company distinguishes itself by industrializing drug discovery through technological innovation, combining diverse scientific disciplines to accelerate development. It maintains strategic collaborations with major pharmaceutical partners including Roche, Sanofi, Bayer, and Takeda.

company_profile.name: Revolution Medicines, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Revolution Medicines, Inc., a clinical-stage precision oncology company, develops novel targeted therapies for RAS-addicted cancers. The company's research and development pipeline consist of RAS(ON) inhibitors that binds RAS variants to be used as monotherapy in combination with other RAS(ON) inhibitors and/or in combination with RAS companion inhibitors or other therapeutic agents, and RAS companion inhibitors to suppress cooperating targets and pathways that sustain RAS-addicted cancers. Its RAS(ON) inhibitors include daraxonrasib (RMC-6236), elironrasib G12C (RMC-6291) which is in early clinical development for the treatment of solid tumors, and zoldonrasib G12D (RMC-9805), which are in phase 3 registrational trial evaluating the combination of daraxonrasib with zoldonrasib in patients with 1L PDAC; and development candidates comprise RMC-5127 (G12V) which is in clinical trial, RMC-0708 (Q61H), that is in clinical trial; and RMC-8839 (G13C). Revolution Medicines, Inc. was incorporated in 2014 and is headquartered in Redwood City, California.
company_profile.description_summary:
A clinical-stage precision oncology company, it develops targeted therapies for RAS-addicted cancers. Its pipeline consists of RAS inhibitors designed as monotherapies or in combination with companion inhibitors and other agents to suppress pathways sustaining these cancers. Lead candidate daraxonrasib is in phase 3 trials for pancreatic cancer.

The company specializes in targeting specific RAS variants, offering precision approaches to previously difficult-to-treat cancers through its innovative RAS(ON) inhibitor platform.

bull_points: Daraxonrasib RASolute 302 Phase 3 trial achieved unprecedented OS benefit (13.2M vs 6.7M months; HR 0.40, p<0.0001) with all primary endpoints met, setting up FDA submission and potential approval in H2 2026—a major near-term catalyst.

Analyst consensus is overwhelmingly bullish with 22 firms rating Strong Buy and an average price target of $182.86, suggesting 12% upside from current $163.76; recent upgrades from JP Morgan (+32.6% PT), Stifel (+100% PT), and RBC Capital (+81.8% PT) signal confidence in trial success.

FDA awarded daraxonrasib Breakthrough Therapy Designation and Orphan Drug Designation for RAS G12+ metastatic pancreatic cancer, plus the company was selected for the FDA Commissioner's National Priority Voucher pilot program—accelerated regulatory pathway increases probability of near-term approval and commercial launch readiness.
bear_points: Q1 2026 EPS of -$2.29 beat expectations of -$1.64 by a wide margin, with R&D and SG&A expenses surpassing estimates by $67.4M and $46.3M respectively—runrate of $1.6–$1.7B operating expenses in 2026 signals uncontrolled cost growth and extended pre-profitability.

Stock has surged ~300% over the past year on trial anticipation; now trading at $163.76 versus intrinsic value of $47.23 (margin of safety -246.7%), creating significant downside risk if daraxonrasib launch disappoints or commercial adoption lags.

Company has zero revenue and negative profitability (ROE -76.67%, profit margin not provided), with a 7.14x current ratio and undisclosed debt metrics masking cash runway concerns; full-year 2026 guidance implies sustained $1.6B+ annual cash burn without product revenue offsetting it.

bull_points: Daraxonrasib addresses a massive market opportunity in RAS-driven cancers—pancreatic cancer alone has >90% RAS mutation rate, and the company is testing the drug across multiple tumor types and settings (second-line, first-line, combination regimens), potentially supporting multi-billion-dollar peak sales.

Strong financial position with a 7.14x current ratio provides runway for aggressive R&D spend; base growth rate of 20.9% (after moderation from financial strength constraints) and 27.9% 5-year EPS CAGR demonstrate the platform's scaling potential as multiple Phase 3 programs mature and achieve regulatory approval.

Royalty Pharma partnership de-risks commercialization with up to $1 billion in milestone payments tied to daraxonrasib approvals and sales thresholds; established sales infrastructure for RAS(ON) inhibitors will support rapid market penetration once approval is secured, reducing capital intensity of launch.
bear_points: Financial strength score of 4/10 and profitability score of 0/10 reflect structural unprofitability and balance sheet constraints—the company will require sustained capital raises or debt financing to support $1.6–$1.7B annual operating expenses for years, diluting shareholders if product revenue lags expectations.

Predictability score of 0 and growth constrained to 20.9% (from unconstrained 27.9%) due to weak financial metrics signal execution risk; ROIC is null and profit margin undefined, indicating no current path to unit economics sustainability—survival depends entirely on successful commercialization of daraxonrasib within a narrow competitive window.

Competitive landscape is intensifying with other RAS inhibitors (Kras, etc.) in clinical development; first-mover advantage with daraxonrasib may erode if competitors achieve similar efficacy or better tolerability, and reimbursement/pricing pressure in oncology threatens peak sales assumptions, undermining the long-term valuation case at 246% premium to intrinsic value.

company_profile.name: rYojbaba Co., Ltd.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: rYojbaba Co., Ltd. provides consulting and health services to various customers in Japan. It operates through two segments: Consulting Services and Health Services. The Consulting Services segment provides labor and corporate consulting services to labor unions, companies to support the practice of whistleblowing and stress checks, and companies and labor unions to resolve disputes. The Health Service segment offers health services through osteopathic clinics and beauty salons to alleviate physical ailments primarily created by work related stress. rYojbaba Co., Ltd. was formerly known as Sakai Seikotsuin Co., Ltd. and changed its name to rYojbaba Co., Ltd. in October 2021. The company was founded in 2015 and is based in Fukuoka City, Japan.
company_profile.description_summary:
This company provides consulting and health services across Japan through two main divisions. The Consulting Services segment offers labor and corporate advisory to unions and companies, supporting whistleblowing practices, stress assessments, and dispute resolution. The Health Services segment operates osteopathic clinics and beauty salons addressing work-related physical ailments.

The company distinctively combines workplace advisory with wellness services, addressing stress-related issues from both organizational and physical health angles.

company_profile.name: Ruanyun Edai Technology Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Ruanyun Edai Technology Inc. operates as a data driven artificial intelligence technology company focused on kindergarten through year twelve education (K-12) in China and Malaysia. The company offers SmartExam solution that assists in delivering China's Academic Proficiency Test, which is required for obtaining a high-school diploma in computer-based format; and SmartHomework system, a digital teaching and learning decision platform that collects paper-based homework data with intelligent scanning machine. It also provides smart enrollment, an intelligent system based on big data, AI, and other information technologies, integrating student status, household registration, property ownership, marriage, leasing, and degree information for comparison, query, and declaration. In addition, the company offers smart evaluation solution, which focuses on a multi-party collaborative education mechanism; smart campus solution that digitally transform various aspects of schools; and digital services to convert paper materials, archives, and exercise book into digital formats, as well as Chinese learning app. Further, it provides self-learning solutions and smart devices, such as smart printers and headsets. Ruanyun Edai Technology Inc. was founded in 2012 and is based in Nanchang, China.
company_profile.description_summary:
This AI-driven technology company serves K-12 education markets in China and Malaysia. It generates revenue primarily through SmartExam, which delivers China's required Academic Proficiency Test in digital format, and SmartHomework, a platform combining intelligent paper-based homework scanning with teaching analytics. Additional offerings include smart enrollment systems, campus digitalization solutions, and learning apps.

The company distinguishes itself through integrated big data and AI applications across education ecosystems, from administrative enrollment to classroom assessment and parent engagement platforms.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 22.8%; DCF methods prioritised

_meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (energy) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 22.5%; DCF methods prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

_meta.type_rationale: Real estate investment trust — P/NAV (P/B) prioritised

_meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (energy) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. This makes earnings-based methods like P/E unreliable, because the earnings figure at any single point in time can be unusually high or low. EV/EBITDA smooths out some of this distortion by looking at earnings before interest and tax, making it a fairer comparison across the economic cycle. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.

company_profile.description_summary:
This financial holding company provides banking and related financial services to individual and corporate customers through three main segments: Corporate Bank, which offers commercial lending and capital markets services; Consumer Bank, which provides retail mortgages and deposit products; and Wealth Management, which delivers investment management and trust services. _meta.type_rationale: Bank or thrift — P/Tangible Book prioritised; DCF excluded
_meta.method_explanation: Banks make money from the difference between what they charge on loans and what they pay on deposits — not from traditional business cash flows. Because of this, standard cash flow models (DCF) do not work well for banks. Instead, we focus on Price-to-Book (P/B), which compares the stock price to the value of the bank's assets on its books. Banks also carry a lot of debt by design — they borrow money to lend it out — so high leverage is normal and not a red flag.
